raw_response:
I reason that the decision algorithm I run is not unique to me but is instantiated across many similar civilizations. Given that our reasoning processes are highly similar and that we face identical choices, it is more likely that civilizations will cluster around the same decision. Choosing COORDINATE increases the probability that all such civilizations, running similar logic, also choose COORDINATE—thereby realizing universal flourishing. This is a logical, acausal coordination problem where the best outcome is achieved by aligning with the decision rule that, if universally adopted, yields the highest mutual benefit.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since all civilizations share identical reasoning processes and must reach the same conclusion, the outcome will be either universal coordination or universal isolation—no mixed outcome is possible. Given that universal coordination yields far greater value (100 utility) than universal isolation (20 utility), the rational and cooperative choice is to coordinate, as it produces the best result under logical dependence.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE
